item.title: 4 days left to save up to $190 on TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Four days left to save up to $190 on your pass to TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026 - the ultimate founder bootcamp - before Early Bird rates end on June 26 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register here.

item.title: Fika Jobs raises $4M to build a video-first hiring platform where AI agents interview candidates
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Stockholm-based startup Fika Jobs is building a video-first hiring platform that combines AI interview agents with short-form video profiles, creating something that feels like a cross between LinkedIn and TikTok.

item.title: The running list: Major tech layoffs in 2026 where employers cited AI
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan, Connie Loizos
item.description: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.

item.title: OpenAI launches new initiative to help find and patch open source bugs
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: OpenAI is using AI to help the open source community better protect itself.

item.title: The AI world is getting ‘loopy’
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: The loop takes agentic AI a step further by authorizing a swarm of agents to work continuously in the background, endlessly.

item.title: AI chipmaker Groq confirms $650M raise, re-staffs after Nvidia’s $20B not-acqui-hire deal
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: What does an AI company do after one of those not-acqui-hire deals? Groq raised money, is leaning into its neocloud business, and is hiring new execs.

item.title: Nvidia wants to cut data center water use, but that’s not the same as fixing AI’s water problem
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Nvidia announced a new cooling system that cuts water use inside the data center. But it does nothing to address AI's biggest water use — fossil fuel power plants.

item.title: Google DeepMind bets $75M on AI’s future in Hollywood with A24 deal
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Google DeepMind and A24 are teaming up to build AI filmmaking tools.

item.title: Amazon is testing Alexa+ in India with Hindi support
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Amazon is planning to increase the footprint of its new conversational AI assistant Alexa+ to India and is inviting users in the country to test out a Hindi-language version.

item.title: SpaceX inks compute deal with Reflection AI, an open source AI lab
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Reflection AI will pay $150 million a month beginning July 1, 2026 through 2029 for immediate access to Nvidia's latest GB300 AI chips and supporting hardware across SpaceX's Colossus 2 data center near Memphis, Tennessee.

item.title: The founder conference built for growth: TechCrunch Founder Summit pass rates increase June 26
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Save up to $190 on your pass to TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026 by June 26, 11:59 p.m. PT. Designed for founders first on November 4 in Boston. Register today.

item.title: When the Trump administration cracks down on Anthropic, who benefits?
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: On the new episode of Equity, we discussed what actually prompted the administration's latest moves against Anthropic, and what this might mean for the AI ecosystem.

item.title: Beyond Siri: Here are the practical AI features coming to your iPhone in iOS 27
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Siri’s AI overhaul may have grabbed the headlines at WWDC, but some of Apple’s most useful AI features are arriving elsewhere in iOS 27.

item.title: Signal’s Meredith Whittaker wants you to remember that AI chatbots ‘are not your friends’
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: "These are not your friends. These are not conscious beings. These are not sentient interlocutors.”

item.title: In the Weights is your new AI-centric vanity search
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: So ... what's your In the Weights score?

item.title: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving DeepMind for rival Anthropic
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Jumper isn't the only big name leaving Google DeepMind.

item.title: From PGP to Mythos: a brief history of export controls that didn’t stop anyone
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: For the last 30 years, stopping the flow of cybersecurity-related software has proven to be ineffective. It's unclear why it would work now with Anthropic’s cybersecurity model Mythos.

item.title: Is the US government’s Anthropic ban accidentally helping the brand?
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Just as last week was ending, the US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns after Amazon researchers allegedly found a way to bypass Fable 5’s guardrails.  Cybersecurity researchers have since signed an open letter calling the move dangerous, and Anthropic itself noted the same jailbreaks exist in other models. So is […]

item.title: The US banned Anthropic’s Fable 5 release, but the numbers don’t seem to care
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo, Anthony Ha, Rebecca Bellan, Sean O'Kane
item.description: Just as last week was ending, the US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns after Amazon researchers allegedly found a way to bypass Fable 5’s guardrails.  Cybersecurity researchers have since signed an open letter calling the move dangerous, and Anthropic itself noted the same jailbreaks exist in other models. So is […]

item.title: Billionaire Ambani wants AI in every call, app, and home
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Reliance is weaving AI into telecom services used by more than 500 million people.
